data.title: Chief Futurist Ian Beacraft to Keynote at Info-Tech LIVE 2026 in Las Vegas on Designing AI-Ready Organizations
data.description: AI is revolutionizing the way value is generated in businesses, yet numerous organizations are functioning with outdated models designed before the AI era. Consequently, merely adopting technology is not leading to lasting effects. The Info-Tech LIVE 2026 IT conference is a major event where these issues will be discussed.

data.title: HelloNation Examines Retirement Savings With Financial Planning Expert Donna Wallace
data.description: A recent publication from HelloNation provides insights on calculating retirement income requirements for ensuring financial security in the long run. The article, released on April 24, 2026, focuses on determining the adequate funds needed to retire with comfort. Located in Southfield, Mich., HelloNation's article offers valuable guidance on planning for retirement and achieving sustainable financial stability. This resource helps individuals understand the monetary essentials for a comfortable retirement, aiding in better financial decision-making.

data.title: Dallas Real Estate Attorney Lynnsee Starr Joins Shackelford, McKinley & Norton
data.description: Dallas-based law firm Shackelford, McKinley & Norton, LLP has welcomed real estate transactions attorney Lynnsee Starr as a new partner in their Dallas office. Additionally, veteran tax attorney David Corkern has returned to the firm. Firm founder John Shackelford praised Starr's skills, highlighting her expertise in navigating challenges within real estate projects. The firm expressed enthusiasm about the new additions to their team.

data.title: 'You're Fooling Yourself,' Top Ripple Exec Says About Alleged Major XRP Announcements
data.description: David Schwartz, Ripple's Chief Technology Officer, rejected claims that the company is orchestrating a widespread strategy for XRP adoption with central banks. He warned investors against buying into conspiracy theories, stating that rumors of undisclosed major announcements with central banks are untrue. Schwartz emphasized that investing based on such speculation is self-deception. He noted that Ripple's partners often require Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDAs) to safeguard their business information, clarifying that NDAs do not imply the speculated coordination with central banks. Schwartz's remarks aimed to dispel unfounded theories and urged investors to exercise caution.

data.title: Woodlands Financial Services Company Announces Second Quarter Cash Dividend
data.description: Yesterday, in Williamsport, Pennsylvania, the Board of Directors of Woodlands Financial Services Company (OTC Pink: WDFN) announced a quarterly cash dividend of twenty-nine cents per share for its common stock. The dividend is set to be paid on May 22, 2026, to shareholders of record as of April 23, 2026.

data.title: These Analysts Revise Their Forecasts On Mobileye After Q1 Results
data.description: Mobileye Global Inc (NASDAQ: MBLY) released its first-quarter financial results, surpassing expectations and adjusting its full-year 2026 revenue outlook. The company reported a 27% increase in revenue to $558 million, exceeding the estimated $515.501 million. Additionally, the adjusted diluted EPS stood at 12 cents, beating the anticipated 9 cents. CEO Professor Amnon Shashua expressed satisfaction with the quarter's performance, attributing it to a robust start to 2026 and favorable demand trends. Furthermore, Mobileye secured a significant design win with Mahindra, expanding its customer base for both Surround ADAS and SuperVision products. The company revised its 2026 revenue guidance to $1.935 billion–$2.015 billion.

data.title: Mineros S.A. Leads Construction of New Public School in Colombia’s Bajo Cauca Region, Advancing Social Investment In Nechí Operations
data.description: In Medellín, Colombia, Mineros S.A. (TSX: MSA | OTCQX: MNSAF | BVC: MINEROS) revealed the initiation of construction for a new public secondary school named Institución Educativa El Bagre in El Bagre, Antioquia. This area falls within the Nechí Alluvial Property operated by the company in the Bajo Cauca region of Colombia. Management stressed the significance of investing in local education, demonstrating a commitment to community development.

data.title: Housing affordability improved for all Californians in 2025, but significant gaps remain wide for Black households, C.A.R. reports
data.description: The affordability gap for Hispanic/Latino households improved, while it worsened for Black households in California. Around 19% of Californians had adequate income to buy a median-priced detached home costing $875,550 in 2025, a slight increase from 18% in 2024. This was one of the findings among different ethnic groups, with shifts in affordability levels.

data.title: ISG to Study Providers of Advanced Analytics and AI Services
data.description: A recent study initiated by ISG in Stamford, Conn., focuses on service providers aiding companies in integrating AI into decision-making processes spanning innovation, customer experience, and operations. This comprehensive research aims to analyze the strategies and capabilities of these providers in utilizing AI technologies effectively. This study will delve into the services offered by these providers to assist companies in leveraging AI solutions for boosting efficiency and productivity across various sectors. Stay tuned for the findings of this study shedding light on the role of service providers in enhancing AI integration in businesses.

data.title: Des Moines FHLB reveals Q1 2026 financial results, declares dividend for shareholders.
data.description: A recent report indicates that the global economy is projected to grow by approximately 5.6% in 2021, as compared to the 3.5% decline it experienced in 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic. The recovery is expected to be uneven across regions, with advanced economies growing by around 5.4% and emerging market and developing economies expanding by about 6%. This growth is mainly attributed to the successful rollout of vaccines, strong fiscal support, and the easing of restrictions in many countries. However, there are concerns about vaccine access disparities and the potential impact of new virus variants on the recovery process. Global trade is also projected to rebound by 6.9% in 2021 after a 5.3% decrease in the previous year. Inflation rates are expected to remain moderate, with advanced economies experiencing an average of 1.7% inflation and emerging markets facing slightly higher rates at around 4.1%. Overall, while there are positive signs of economic recovery, challenges remain in ensuring an inclusive and sustainable growth path for all countries.
